title: The Operating System: Kernel, System Calls, Drivers, Processes and Memory Segmentation
subtopics: Kernel functions (process, memory, file system, IPC)

definitions.description: A program that manages computer resources, links hardware to applications, provides services to applications, and supervises their execution.
The core of the OS: manages hardware and software resources, runs in kernel mode, and provides process management, memory management, file system, and IPC.
Unrestricted CPU execution mode used by the OS; allows access to all hardware.
Restricted CPU execution mode used by applications; limited memory access and no direct hardware access.
A mechanism by which a user-mode application requests a service from the kernel, implemented via a software interrupt.
An instance of a running program with its own memory space (code, data, stack); the OS schedules processes on the CPU.
A sub-unit of a process; threads within the same process share the process's memory space but have their own execution context.
A data structure maintained by the OS that stores start address, size, and access rights of a memory segment.
A memory management scheme that divides the address space into variable-size segments; virtual address = segment selector + offset within segment.
Intel x86 access control: CPL (process level), RPL (selector level), DPL (segment level); access allowed when CPL ≤ DPL and RPL ≤ DPL.

algorithms.name: System call sequence
steps: Software interrupt is generated; CPU switches to kernel mode
Kernel identifies the requested service and dispatches the routine
Results placed in accessible memory zone
CPU switches back to user mode; program resumes

algorithms.name: Memory access via segmentation
steps: Check access rights; trap on insufficient privileges
Check offset against segment size; trap if exceeded

examples.name: printf with buffered system calls
examples.description: printf builds text in a local buffer; write() system call is issued only when buffer is full, reducing expensive kernel transitions.

examples.name: Segmentation illustration
examples.description: Descriptor table with 5 entries; mov byte ptr ds:[eax],25 with ds=3 → physical address 155000+27348=182348.

examples.name: Intel privilege access condition
examples.description: Access allowed iff CPL ≤ DPL AND RPL ≤ DPL.

learning_objectives: Describe the role and main components of an operating system
Explain the distinction between kernel mode and user mode
Trace a complete system call from application to kernel and back
Explain the role and structure of device drivers
Describe process states and multitasking mechanisms
Explain virtual vs. physical addresses and the role of the MMU
Describe the memory segmentation mechanism and segment descriptors
Explain Intel privilege levels and the access control rule
